title: Trump, Defiant After Bad Week, Pushes Ahead on Politically Unpopular Ideas
summary: President Trump continues to act like he’s politically all-powerful, even in the face of indications that he is not.

title: Green Card Seekers Must Leave U.S. to Apply, Trump Administration Says
summary: The change is likely to affect hundreds of thousands of people. It could also lead to more family separations as spouses or relatives wait for application decisions, immigration lawyers said.

summary: Secretary of State Marco Rubio has a “gargantuan task” during his visit to Delhi: defuse tensions over President Trump’s anti-India aggression and overtures to China.

title: White House Approves $9 Billion for Spy Agencies to Catch Up on A.I.
summary: The C.I.A. and N.S.A. cannot fully deploy the latest models on their classified systems because of a shortage of cutting-edge chips.

summary: As people across the Middle East braced for the possibility of renewed fighting, officials from both sides said there were signs that they were moving closer to an agreement.

title: The Risks of Iran’s Threat to Control the Strait of Hormuz
summary: Iran is trying to assert its control over the strait by charging for passage. Experts say it is unlikely to happen, but the threat has unsettled the shipping industry.

title: Israeli Strikes Pummel Lebanon, Killing Medics Amid Fragile Truce
summary: Saturday’s strikes damaged a main hospital in the Lebanese city of Tyre, as funerals for paramedics killed a day earlier were held.

summary: In the relative calm of the early days of a cease-fire, there were heart-rending scenes as people exhumed their loved ones from temporary graves and reburied them.

title: Short Naps, Long Hours: How Autism Clinics Squeeze Medicaid Dollars Out of Preschoolers
summary: The industry has grown rapidly, straining state budgets. A focus on finances has led to overbilling, fraud and even harm.

title: 5 Takeaways From a Times Investigation on Autism Therapy Clinics
summary: A rapidly growing industry often overprescribes treatment to young children with autism, who spend as many as 40 hours a week at the facilities.

title: Garden Grove Chemical Tank Could Be Inching Toward Explosion, Authorities Say
summary: Firefighters still have few options to prevent a tank of toxic chemicals in Garden Grove, Calif., from leaking or exploding.

title: Garden Grove Chemical Plant Crisis: What to Know and Evacuation Map
summary: Firefighters continue to cool a chemical storage tank with water to prevent it from breaching or exploding. Some 40,000 residents near the Orange County plant have been evacuated to shelters.

title: U.S. Ebola Travel Ban Faces Criticism From Congo Health Officials
summary: Kinshasa residents continue to pack markets, bars and public transportation, despite growing international concern about the spread of the virus.

title: Mob Burns Congo Ebola Center Amid Rare Strain Outbreak
summary: Several hundred people massed at the gates of a hospital to demand the body of a suspected Ebola victim. Violence broke out when staff refused the request.

title: White House Tells Congo’s Soccer Team to Isolate, Citing Ebola Outbreak
summary: The team, now in Belgium, must isolate there for 21 days or risk being denied permission to come to Houston for the World Cup, an official said.

summary: Voters want to dictate which offices their preferred politicians hold, what positions they take, and even where and how they live. It is fantasy football: political edition.

title: Track Changes
